title: Avoid throwing exceptions in the main method
description: There's no reason for a main method to throw anything. Instead, the method should gracefully handle any exceptions that may bubble up to it, attach as much contextual information as possible, and perform whatever logging or user communication is necessary, and exit with a non-zero (i.e. non-success) exit code if necessary.
recommendation: Handle exceptions within the main method and exit with a non-zero exit code if necessary.

checks.message: Handle exceptions within the main method and exit with a non-zero exit code if necessary.
